article.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 40 points, 11 assists and seven rebounds and the NBA-leading Oklahoma City Thunder withstood multiple injury-related absences to trounce the Milwaukee Bucks 122-102 on Wednesday night.
article.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 40 points as short-handed Thunder roll past Bucks 122-102

images.caption: Oklahoma City Thunder vs. Milwaukee Bucks: Game Highlights
images.name: Oklahoma City Thunder vs. Milwaukee Bucks: Game Highlights

article.linkText: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 40 points as short-handed Thunder roll past Bucks 122-102
article.story: MILWAUKEE -- — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 40 points, 11 assists and seven rebounds and the NBA-leading Oklahoma City Thunder withstood multiple injury-related absences to trounce the Milwaukee Bucks 122-102 on Wednesday night. The Thunder won for the seventh time in eight games despite playing without Jalen Williams (strained right hamstring), Isaiah Hartenstein (strained right soleus), Alex Caruso (strained right adductor), Aaron Wiggins (sore right groin) and Jaylin Williams (bruised left glute). Gilgeous-Alexander picked up the slack, as the reigning MVP shot 16 of 19 from the floor. According to Sportradar, this marked just the second time since at least the 1980-81 season that a player had at least 40 points and 10 assists in a game while shooting 80% or better. Coincidentally, the other instance was just a day earlier, when Toronto’s Immanuel Quickley had 40 points and 10 assists while shooting 11 of 13 in the Raptors’ 145-127 victory over the Golden State Warriors on Tuesday. Oklahoma City’s Ajay Mitchell scored 18 points in the first half and ended up playing 25 minutes before leaving with a hip bruise. Kenrich Williams also scored 18 points for the Thunder, who shot 54.4% from the floor. Milwaukee's Giannis Antetokounmpo had 19 points, 14 rebounds and seven assists. The Bucks were missing Myles Turner due to a sprained left ankle and Kevin Porter Jr. because of a strained oblique. Bucks coach Doc Rivers said before the game that Turner was day to day, but that Porter is “not going to play anytime soon.” Porter's injury resulted in more playing time for Cole Anthony, who responded with 17 points off the bench. Bobby Portis added 15 points and a career-high nine assists. Milwaukee lost despite getting 46 points from its bench. Oklahoma City scored the game’s first seven points and never looked back while closing a four-game trip in which it went 3-1. Milwaukee committed eight of its 16 turnovers in the first quarter as the Thunder built a 38-18 lead. The margin never dropped below 14 the rest of the way as Oklahoma City led by as many as 25. Up next Thunder: Host the Indiana Pacers on Friday. Bucks: Host the Denver Nuggets on Friday. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA

images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Luka Doncic #77 of the Los Angeles Lakers handles the ball during the game against the Miami Heat on March 19, 2026 at Kaseya Center in Miami, Florida.
images.caption: The Lakers' championship odds are on the move again after Luka Doncic's injury.
images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.alt: Los Angeles Lakers forward LeBron James (23) scores on a breakaway dunk during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Orlando Magic, Saturday, March 21, 2026, in Orlando, Fla.
images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are on a roll as March winds down.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.

videos.description: Ajay Mitchell hits a 3-pointer to extend the Thunder's lead over the Bucks.
videos.headline: Ajay Mitchell cashes a 3

videos.description: Bobby Portis comes up with a steal, then gets an and-1 on his layup.
videos.headline: Bobby Portis gets the steal and layup

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Bobby Portis gets the steal and layup) 2026/01/22 ESHEET

videos.description: Ajay Mitchell makes a tough floater to give OKC a 21-point lead.
videos.headline: Ajay Mitchell sinks a floater

videos.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander puts up 40 points, 11 assists and seven rebounds in the Thunder’s 122-102 win over the Bucks.
videos.headline: SGA drops 40 in Thunder's rout of Bucks

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (SGA drops 40 in Thunder's rout of Bucks) 2026/01/22 ESHEET
